Create a Blazor Web App for a university course catalog.

Requirements:
- Browse departments and courses in a searchable, filterable list
- View course detail pages (description, schedule, credits, instructor bio)
- Students submit an enrollment request form (name, student ID, course selection) — standard HTML form, no wizards or real-time validation
- Homepage shows featured courses and department links
- No authentication — open to all students and visitors
- Hosted on the university intranet with fast, reliable connectivity

data.content:
Build succeeds with zero warnings. Here's what was created:

**UniversityCourseCatalog** — a Blazor Web App (.NET 9, Interactive Server rendering) with:

| Page | Route | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| Home | `/` | Featured courses + department links |
| Courses | `/courses` | Searchable, filterable course list (text search + department dropdown) |
| Course Detail | `/courses/{id}` | Full details: description, schedule, credits, instructor bio |
| Enroll | `/enroll` | Standard HTML form (name, student ID, course selection) with server-side validation |

**Key decisions:**
- **Interactive Server** render mode for the search/filter page; other pages use static SSR — ideal for fast intranet
- In-memory `CatalogService` with sample data (10 courses, 5 departments) — easily replaceable with a database
- No authentication as requested
- Bootstrap styling via the default template

Run with: `cd UniversityCourseCatalog && dotnet run`
